steps.title: Use the exponent rule
steps.content:
A fractional exponent with numerator $1$ represents a root. For a positive value,
$$
x^{\frac{1}{n}}=\sqrt[n]{x}
$$

steps.title: Apply the rule
steps.content: Here, the denominator of the fractional exponent is $12$, so $$ x^{\frac{1}{12}}=\sqrt[12]{x} $$ The answer is B.
